steps.title: Identify the crucial evidence needed
steps.content: The student claims modern irrigation methods have a 'lineage that long predates' the Bear River Reservoir's 1900 completion. To support this claim, the quotation must show that today's southwestern irrigation practices use principles or descend from methods developed well before 1900 — not merely that irrigation is important or modern.

steps.title: Match to the historical evidence
steps.content: Choice D describes the Hohokam irrigation system developed in the 7th century CE and explicitly notes its “hydraulic engineering design principles are still utilized by today's engineers” — directly connecting ancient practice to modern use. The other choices describe 20th-century irrigation developments (A focuses on post-WWII increases, C identifies sprinkler systems as late-20th-century innovations) or discuss current irrigation importance without establishing historical descent (B). This matches choice D — D is correct.
